source_jobs.title: Controller
source_jobs.description:
Teamworks is a late-stage, high-growth sports technology company serving professional leagues, collegiate athletic departments, Olympic programs, and military organizations worldwide. We're scaling fast, navigating M&A activity, and building toward significant financial milestones. The accounting function needs to keep pace — and that means bringing in a Controller who can own it.


THE ROLE

 - Own the monthly close process end-to-end — identify opportunities to make it faster and more accurate, then execute on them

 - Lead all accounting workstreams through M&A activity, including purchase accounting (ASC 805), opening balance sheets, and financial integration — this is active, recurring work across multiple deals, not a one-time event

 - Manage multi-entity consolidations, multi-currency accounting, and international statutory compliance across a footprint that continues to grow through acquisition

 - Build and strengthen internal controls, systems, and processes that scale with the company's growth — and position us well for future audit and financing events

 - Drive cross-functional partnership with FP&A, RevOps, Legal, and functional leadership — translating complex accounting topics into clear financial insights that inform real decisions

 - Develop and grow the accounting team, building toward the scale and structure the business needs


WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING

 - Active CPA license

 - 6–10 years of progressive accounting experience, including meaningful time in public accounting — Big 4 or large regional firm strongly preferred

 - Prior experience as a Controller or Assistant Controller, preferably at a SaaS company

 - Hands-on M&A experience, specifically purchase accounting (ASC 805)

 - Strong technical accounting knowledge across ASC 606, ASC 718, ASC 350, multi-entity consolidations, and international accounting

 - Experience building or materially improving internal controls in a scaling company

 - Experience leading an external audit process — you've owned the relationship with auditors and managed the process end-to-end

 - People management experience — you've led or mentored an accounting team

 - ERP proficiency, Intacct experience strongly preferred


EVEN BETTER IF

 - You've been through a financing event — Series D+, a debt raise, or IPO readiness — and know what it takes to get a company prepared

 - You’ve worked directly with lenders or investors on reporting requirements and covenant compliance

 - You have experience integrating financial systems and collaborating closely with FP&A on planning and reporting

 - You’ve managed international statutory compliance across multiple jurisdictions


WHY THIS ROLE

Most Controller roles ask you to maintain the books. This one asks you to build something. With ongoing M&A activity, an external audit to lead, international expansion in the picture, and a cross-functional leadership team that genuinely wants a seat at the table for Finance — the scope here is real and it grows with you. This is a rare chance to shape how a finance function is built from the inside.



This role is not eligible for sponsorship at this time.


To all recruitment agencies: Teamworks does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Teamworks employees or any other organization location. Teamworks is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.



ABOUT TEAMWORKS

We're the Operating System for Sports™, powering 7,000+ organizations worldwide, from collegiate programs to every major pro league. Founded in 2006, we've evolved from a messaging tool for college football into the leading sports tech platform, with 600+ global teammates building the future of sports tech. Our solutions span Personnel, Coaching, Performance, Operations, and Intelligence - helping teams recruit smarter, train better, stay compliant, and win.

Teamworks is an equal opportunity employer - if you live our core values every day and

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Product Success Manager, Nutrition (Pro & Enterprise)
source_jobs.description:
I'm Matt Crawley https://www.linkedin.com/in/matthewcrawley, Manager of Product Success, Pro/Enterprise at Teamworks. My team owns the implementation and ongoing success of our Nutrition product across professional and enterprise sports organizations. We work with organizations ranging from UFC and WWE to NFL, NHL, MLB, and NBA franchises, national sporting federations, and Olympic committees, and we're growing the team to deepen our coverage of sports nutrition.

We're hiring a Product Success Manager to join this team, anchored in our Nutrition product. You'll bring a dietitian's perspective to the role, understanding firsthand how nutrition staff operates inside elite sports organizations, and you'll own your accounts, drive adoption, build relationships with performance and nutrition staff, and ensure the organizations we serve are getting real value from the platform.


THE ROLE

 - Own a portfolio of partner accounts across professional and enterprise sports organizations, managing all touchpoints from implementation and onboarding through check-ins, syncs, recaps, and case-by-case problem-solving

 - Lead end-to-end implementation and adoption strategy for the Nutrition product, with enough working knowledge of the broader Performance suite to support teammates and accounts as needed

 - Build trust-based relationships with dietitians, performance directors, and medical staff, with enough understanding of nutrition workflows to drive real platform value rather than surface-level adoption

 - Guide organizations in establishing data governance frameworks, workflow standards, and nutrition dashboards that translate complex dietary and performance data into actionable insights for coaches, performance staff, and front office decision-makers

 - Act as the voice of the customer by sharing feedback and insights across Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to inform the product roadmap and improve the experience for the organizations we serve

 - Leverage AI tools to improve the quality and efficiency of your work, contributing to Teamworks' broader initiative around AI-supported operations

 - Monitor account health in Gainsight, flag critical alerts proactively, and develop strategies for renewal and product growth across your portfolio


WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING

 - Experience as a Registered Dietitian (RD/RDN) working within professional, enterprise, or high-performance sports organizations, with a clear understanding of how elite sports organizations operate

 - Deep knowledge of sports nutrition and dietitian workflows at the elite level, including menu planning, supplementation, travel nutrition, and athlete-facing nutrition education

 - Demonstrated ability to navigate organizational complexity and build consensus across diverse stakeholder groups, from front office executives to coaching staff to medical and nutrition personnel

 - Proven cross-functional collaboration skills, with experience working alongside Product, Engineering, or equivalent technical teams to troubleshoot and build solutions

 - Hands-on experience with the Teamworks Nutrition product or a comparable platform used in professional or collegiate sports


EVEN BETTER IF

 - Exposure to or working knowledge of the broader Teamworks Performance ecosystem, including High Performance and Strength and Conditioning

 - Active use of AI tools in day-to-day workflow, with the ability to speak to how that has changed the way you work

 - Experience spanning more than one performance domain, for example, a dietitian with a sports science or strength and conditioning background


WHY THIS ROLE

This role gives you a portfolio of recognizable organizations across elite sports rather than a single program to manage. You'll build relationships across one of the largest operating networks in sports technology, bring back best practices from across the industry, and work inside a company that is actively investing in AI as a competitive adv

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer I
source_jobs.description:
I'm Scott Roberts https://www.linkedin.com/in/scottrobertsprofile/, Senior Manager, Engineering at Teamworks, and I lead the Data Platform team. We're building the lakehouse and data pipelines that consolidate performance data and product telemetry across athletic and tactical divisions, the connective tissue behind cross-product analytics, GenAI features, and customer-facing insights.

There's meaningful work to own from day one, and I'm hiring a Data Engineer to join us at the build-and-ship layer and grow alongside the platform You'll work on the same stack and toward the same goals as our senior engineers, with scope calibrated to where you are now and clear room to grow into bigger ownership over time.


THE ROLE

 - Build and maintain production data pipelines that move performance and product data into the lakehouse with documented schemas, tests, and observability

 - Contribute to platform code and Terraform-based infrastructure under the guidance of senior engineers, applying standard patterns and best practices

 - Partner with product engineering and domain SMEs to design schema conventions and integrate new data sources cleanly

 - Take ownership of components you build, including rotating on-call coverage, responding within SLOs, and contributing runbooks and post-incident learnings

 - Anticipate where pipelines can break, build in graceful failure handling, and raise reliability for the components you own


WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING

 - 2+ years of professional experience in data engineering, software engineering, or a closely related role building production systems

 - Hands-on experience writing and shipping production-quality Python code, with a track record of code that's been reviewed, tested, and deployed

 - Working knowledge of SQL and modern data warehouse or lakehouse concepts (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Redshift, Delta Lake, Iceberg)

 - Exposure to AWS and willingness to grow into infrastructure-as-code practices using Terraform

 - Strong troubleshooting instincts and clear judgment on when to ask for help vs. push through

 - Comfortable participating in a rotating on-call schedule


EVEN BETTER IF

 - You've worked with Spark, Databricks, Trino, dbt, or Airflow in production

 - You have exposure to lakehouse architectures (Delta, Iceberg, Hudi) or modern data observability tooling

 - You've actively used AI tools (Claude, Cursor, Copilot) for spec-driven development, code, tests, or troubleshooting

 - You have experience working with sports performance data or understand the nuances of modeling athletic datasets

 - You have a Bachelor's Degree or higher in Computer Science


WHY THIS ROLE

You'll be building real production data infrastructure that customers, including professional teams, college programs, and military organizations, depend on every day. You'll learn fast alongside senior engineers who invest in mentorship and craft, with visible ownership of pipelines that matter from the first months in. This is a strong launching pad for an early-career engineer who wants to grow inside a meaningful platform buildout.



This role is not eligible for sponsorship at this time.


To all recruitment agencies: Teamworks does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Teamworks employees or any other organization location. Teamworks is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Our solutions span Personnel, Coaching, Performance, Operations, and Intelligence - helping teams recruit smarter, train better, stay compliant, and win.

source_jobs.title: Computer Vision Researcher
source_jobs.description:
I'm Jordan Chipka https://www.linkedin.com/in/jordan-chipka/, Sr. Engineering Manager at Teamworks, and I'm hiring alongside Ryan Sanford https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryansanford2/, one of our Engineering Managers on the Coaching Engineering team. Our Computer Vision team powers the Teamworks Coaching product line — built on the foundation we've been developing since our acquisition of Telemetry Sports and strengthened through the additions of Sportlogiq, Zelus Analytics, and PFF — and we're expanding it to deliver player tracking and performance analytics across multiple sports at professional and collegiate scale.

This is a role for someone who wants to do real applied research and ship it. You'll work on a small, highly technical team where the problems are genuinely hard, the resources are there to solve them, and the output ends up in the hands of players, coaches, and front office personnel at some of the most competitive sports programs in the world.

We're open to hiring this role in Eastern Time or Central Time in the US or Canada, and at either the Computer Vision Researcher I or II levels.


THE ROLE

 - Own the development and deployment of advanced CV models — object detection, multi-object tracking, camera calibration, and semantic segmentation — for player tracking across multiple sports

 - Build and scale high-performance pipelines for video and image data ingestion, processing, and analysis that hold up under the demands of professional and collegiate programs

 - Contribute across the full CV/ML lifecycle, from data gathering, model development and training through production deployment, monitoring, and iterative refinement

 - Drive R&D initiatives including 3D body pose tracking, real-time analysis systems, and integration with LLM/NLP research — with real latitude to explore within the scope of each project

 - Collaborate closely with Software Engineering, Product, and Data Ops to ensure your work is understood, integrated, and used correctly downstream


WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING

 - Hands-on proficiency in Python, PyTorch, and OpenCV for production CV applications — not just research prototypes

 - Demonstrated experience taking CV/ML models through the full lifecycle: development, training, evaluation, deployment, and ongoing refinement in real-world systems

 - Ability to design and implement modular, maintainable software architectures for complex CV/ML pipelines — this team builds systems, not just models

 - Cloud computing experience with AWS or similar platforms, plus working knowledge of Linux environments and CUDA for GPU optimization

 - The ability to communicate complex technical work clearly to non-technical partners — when other teams act on your outputs, they need to actually understand what you built and why


EVEN BETTER IF

 - Experience with multi-camera systems, 3D pose estimation, or camera calibration for spatial tracking

 - Background in real-time or near-real-time CV system development with performance optimization

 - Research publication record in CV or ML, open-source contributions, or prior work in sports analytics


WHY THIS ROLE

This team has meaningful research latitude — you're not just implementing someone else's architecture. Teamworks invests heavily in AI tooling and makes sure engineers have what they need to work at the frontier. If you're excited about the intersection of software and sports, there aren't many places doing this work at this scale.



This role is not eligible for sponsorship at this time.


To all recruitment agencies: Teamworks does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Teamworks employees or any other organization location. Teamworks is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.



ABOUT TEAMWORKS

We're the Operating System for Sports™, powering 7,000+ organizations worldwide, from collegiate programs to every major pro league. Founded in 2006, we've evolved from a messa

source_jobs.title: Staff Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
I'm Scott Roberts https://www.linkedin.com/in/scottrobertsprofile/, Senior Manager, Engineering at Teamworks. I lead the Data Platform team, and we're building the foundation that brings together athlete performance data, product telemetry, and the unique datasets we've accumulated through several acquisitions in the sports tech space. Right now, a lot of that data lives in disparate systems and original tech stacks, and much of it isn't yet defined or organized well enough for us to fully leverage it for analytics, ML, and the AI features we're building. My team is changing that by building a modern lakehouse that becomes the backbone of our cross-product analytics, ML, and AI, with just enough structure and ownership to move us up a level in data maturity.

This is where you come in. I'm looking for a Staff Data Engineer who can co-define the technical direction of this platform, establish the standards other engineers build on, and make architectural decisions that will matter for years. You will be strategic and hands-on, as comfortable shaping the roadmap and bringing other leaders and teams along as you are writing the Python and building the pipelines. The work is highly visible, organizationally backed, and tied directly to capabilities that show up on the field for athletes and coaches.


THE ROLE

 - Define the technical architecture and platform standards for our lakehouse on AWS: distributed cloud architecture, schema conventions, multi-tenant isolation, and integration design

 - Lead design and delivery of the production pipelines that consolidate performance and product data, and own data modeling for complex entities (time-series, hierarchical, multi-source) so the models serve products, analytics, and ML

 - Introduce just enough data governance, ownership, and stewardship to raise our data maturity, and lay the catalog and semantic-layer foundation that analytics, ML, and AI agents can reason over

 - Author and maintain the Data Platform playbook (reusable patterns, ADRs, runbooks, Terraform modules) with data quality and reliability built in, so product teams can self-serve new datasets and integrations

 - Lead delivery end to end, from requirements and planning through coordinating workstreams and translating status to senior leadership and non-technical partners

 - Mentor engineers across levels, raise the bar through design review and on-call ownership, and be the engineering voice shaping the platform roadmap


WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING

 - 10+ years of data engineering or related experience, with strong Python for pipelines, transformations, and platform tooling

 - Deep expertise designing, operating, and setting direction for lakehouse platforms (Delta Lake, Iceberg, or Hudi) and modern processing engines (Spark, Databricks, Trino, or Snowflake) at production scale, with the judgment to make the hard tradeoffs and troubleshoot them

 - Expert AWS and distributed cloud architecture experience (S3, IAM, Glue, EMR/Lambda, networking), fluent writing Terraform and the best practices for implementing those designs

 - Deep data modeling and schema design for complex entities (time-series, hierarchical, multi-source) in multi-tenant environments, across multiple systems you've built (warehouses, lakehouses, relational), plus proven integration standards across teams (event-driven, API, batch)

 - Track record of standing up or significantly maturing a data platform from ambiguous goals, including the organizational work of aligning leaders and teams and communicating decisions to senior and non-technical stakeholders through RFCs and ADRs

 - Familiarity with how data governance, ownership, and stewardship programs are introduced, and the judgment to apply just enough to raise data maturity without over-engineering it


EVEN BETTER IF

 - You have sports industry experience and have used a lakehouse to ingest multi-source performance data (Catapult, Vald, Kinexon) and model it for produc

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer II
source_jobs.description:
I'm Angelo Pace https://www.linkedin.com/in/angelo-pace-7b050a14/, Senior Manager, Platform at Teamworks. Our Platform Engineering team owns the infrastructure that keeps elite sports organizations running, and we're expanding that foundation into high-performance tactical environments. I'm looking for a Platform Engineer II to support day-to-day operations of our AWS GovCloud infrastructure and help us drive meaningful improvements to observability and stability as we grow.

This is a collaborative, hands-on role. You'll work alongside a platform team that serves application teams across the organization, and the best work you'll do here is the kind that makes those teams' lives measurably easier.


THE ROLE

 - Own day-to-day operations and maintenance of our AWS GovCloud environment, including EKS cluster management, EC2, RDS/Aurora, ElastiCache, S3, and IAM

 - Manage deployments of new features and fixes across commercial and GovCloud environments, coordinating with application teams through structured intake and ad hoc request processes

 - Build and maintain reusable Terraform modules, Helm charts, and CI/CD pipeline templates (GitLab CI) that enforce organizational standards for scalability, compliance, and observability

 - Diagnose cross-cutting infrastructure issues — networking misconfigurations, IAM permission boundaries, Kubernetes scheduling failures — evaluating blast radius and downstream impact before proposing solutions

 - Partner with application teams to translate platform changes and new standards into clear, actionable guidance that reduces friction across the organization

 - Drive improvements to environment observability and stability using Datadog, and participate in on-call rotation with full production ownership


WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING

 - 3+ years of experience in platform, infrastructure, or DevOps engineering

 - Strong proficiency in Linux administration in production environments, including RHEL-based system management

 - Proven hands-on experience with Kubernetes (EKS preferred), Terraform, and CI/CD pipelines using GitLab CI or comparable tooling

 - Experience deploying and managing infrastructure in AWS

 - Strong cross-functional communication skills, with the ability to translate platform changes into clear guidance for application teams


EVEN BETTER IF

 - You have experience working in AWS GovCloud, FedRAMP, or IL4-regulated environments

 - You have a background in defense contracting or government environments

 - You have experience with configuration management and automation tooling (Ansible, AWX, and Packer)

 - You're familiar with Python or shell scripting for operational tooling, or have experience with Datadog or a comparable observability platform


WHY THIS ROLE

Platform Engineering at Teamworks sits at the foundation of everything we build and operate, and this team is expanding into one of the most consequential verticals we serve. The person in this role will have real ownership over systems that matter, with a team that values good engineering and clear communication in equal measure.



This role is not eligible for sponsorship at this time.


To all recruitment agencies: Teamworks does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Teamworks employees or any other organization location. Teamworks is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Our solutions span Personnel, Coaching, Performance, Operations, and Intelligence - helping teams recruit smarter, train better, stay compliant, and win.

Teamworks is an equal opportunity employer - if you live our core values every day

source_jobs.title: Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
I'm Irina Juchimiuk Jaworski https://linkedin.com/in/irinajuchimiukjaworski/?skipRedirect=true, Director of Marketing, International Sport at Teamworks, where I lead marketing across EMEA, APAC, and LATAM.

Every day, I have the opportunity to work at the intersection of sports and technology, helping some of the world's leading sports organisations unlock new ways to perform, connect, and succeed. Our team drives marketing strategy across some of the most diverse, dynamic, and fast-growing sports markets in the world.

As our EMEA footprint continues to expand, we're looking for a talented Marketing Manager to join our team, to take full ownership of the region's marketing execution while also helping shape where we go next. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who is passionate about sports, thrives in a fast-paced environment, and wants to make a real impact within the global leader in sports technology. This is not a role for someone who wants to manage a tidy list of tasks. It is for someone who thrives in complexity, moves fast, brings creative ideas to life, and holds themselves to a high standard across everything they deliver.

If you're excited by innovation, enjoy collaborating across teams, and want to help shape the future of sport, we'd love to hear from you.


THE ROLE

 - Own the planning and execution of multi-channel demand generation campaigns across EMEA, covering priority markets including the UK, DACH, Middle East, and Southern Europe

 - Generate pipeline opportunities for the Sales team by creating compelling content, delivering engaging webinars and events, and partnering closely with Business Development to execute targeted outreach campaigns that resonate with key audiences and drive demand

 - Lead the organisation and delivery of owned and third-party events, from customer conferences and networking sponsorships to online webinars, ensuring every activation reflects the quality and ambition of the Teamworks brand

 - Support the go-to-market team in expanding into new markets and launching products across EMEA. Identify cross-sell opportunities and translate product value into compelling campaigns that resonate with prospects and customers in each region.

 - Create detailed marketing briefs and manage campaign execution across email, paid, social, and web, working closely with designers, strategists, and product marketers to bring campaigns from idea to delivery


WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING

 - 5+ years of B2B demand generation or integrated campaign experience, with a track record of managing multiple projects simultaneously to a high standard

 - Based in the UK

 - End-to-end campaign ownership — From strategy to execution, and back again. You identify the opportunity, build the brief, write the copy, set up the landing page, track the RSVP, and report the results — then dig into what worked, what didn't, and why. You don't hand off; you ship, learn, and sharpen.

 - Proven experience organising owned, 3rd party events and webinars — You've run end-to-end B2B webinars including speaker coordination, registration flows, timing strategy by timezone, and post-event follow-up.

 - You’re comfortable working across HubSpot, Figma, AI tools, and Notion, and are always looking for ways to leverage technology to work more efficiently and deliver higher-quality results.

 - A genuine understanding of the sports industry, its trends, its organisations, and what motivates the people who work within it.

 - Exceptional written English, with the ability to create compelling marketing copy independently.

 - Cross-functional comfort — You work with Product Marketing, Sales, CS, Product, and regional teams without needing heavy coordination.

 - AI-augmented workflow — You use AI actively as a creative and operational collaborator — for drafting, iterating, building tools, and accelerating repeatable tasks. You're not just AI-curious; you're AI-productive.


EVEN BETTER IF

 - You have

source_jobs.title: Payroll Manager
source_jobs.description:
I'm Kristen Nolan https://www.linkedin.com/in/kristenenolan/, Vice President, People & Accounting at Teamworks. We're a global company with employees across the US, Canada, UK, Netherlands, Australia, and Belgium, and our payroll operations need to keep pace with where we're headed. Right now that means bringing on someone who can own this function end-to-end, not just execute the cycles but build the infrastructure that scales with us.

This role sits at the intersection of People and Finance, which means the work you do touches every employee at the company. I'm looking for someone who takes that seriously, brings sharp attention to detail, and can improve how we operate over time, not just maintain the status quo.


THE ROLE

 - Own end-to-end payroll operations across all global entities (US, Canada, UK, Netherlands, Australia, and Belgium), plus EOR payroll through Deel for additional international locations

 - Drive payroll compliance across all regions, including tax filings, required reporting, and year-end close (W-2s, T4s, and local equivalents)

 - Build and maintain the controls, reconciliations, and documentation that keep payroll audit-ready and scalable as we add new countries and entities

 - Partner with People and Finance to ensure clean inputs, accurate journal entries, and meaningful payroll reporting that supports budgeting and forecasting

 - Own the employee-facing side of payroll, triaging the inbox, resolving issues quickly, and managing benefits and retirement deductions (401(k), RRSP, and regional equivalents) with accuracy


WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING

 - 5+ years owning end-to-end payroll, including hands-on experience running US and Canada payroll specifically

 - Proven track record supporting global payroll across multiple countries and entities simultaneously

 - Deep knowledge of payroll principles and multi-jurisdictional tax and labor law (local, state, federal, and international)

 - Proficiency in payroll software and systems, with experience administering retirement and benefits programs

 - A customer-service mindset when supporting employees, paired with the attention to detail and judgment needed to meet tight deadlines without errors


EVEN BETTER IF

 - You've worked in Rippling

 - You've managed payroll through Deel or another EOR provider

 - You have experience with payroll accounting, reconciliations, and payroll-related journal entries


WHY THIS ROLE

Teamworks is growing in headcount and geographic complexity, and the person in this role will build the payroll foundation that supports all of it. This isn't a maintenance job — there's real infrastructure to improve, new entities to onboard, and a seat at the table with People and Finance leadership to do it right.



This role is not eligible for sponsorship at this time.


To all recruitment agencies: Teamworks does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Teamworks employees or any other organization location. Teamworks is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Specialist – Performance (North America)
source_jobs.description:
I'm Matt Hamm https://www.linkedin.com/in/matt-hamm-15431a50/, Manager of Customer Support at Teamworks. Teamworks Performance is one of the fastest-growing product categories in sports tech, and the demand for world-class support to match is exploding. As a Customer Support Specialist focused on Performance, you'll be the behind-the-scenes engine that keeps elite sports organizations running at full speed. Working across our AMS, Nutrition, and Strength & Conditioning products, you'll own the resolution of complex, time-sensitive challenges for some of the most sophisticated performance teams in professional and collegiate athletics.

This isn't a traditional support role. You'll work autonomously, manage your own queue, and partner closely with Product Success Managers and Customer Success Managers to deliver the kind of bespoke service that makes Teamworks a true competitive advantage for our customers. You'll bring a performance background, a data-literate mind, and the problem-solving instincts to navigate complex questions without always having a complete picture.

This is an excellent opportunity to join one of the fastest-growing companies in sports tech and grow your career on the front lines of the Performance category.


THE ROLE

 - Become deeply knowledgeable about the Teamworks product suite, with a specialty in Teamworks AMS, Nutrition, and Strength & Conditioning, and the technologies used to troubleshoot and prioritize issues.

 - Own a queue of support tickets across AMS, Nutrition, and Strength & Conditioning, triaging and resolving issues based on severity and business impact.

 - Gain an understanding of our customers' challenges and needs in order to troubleshoot and solve complex problems in a prompt and friendly manner.

 - Fulfill technical requests including workflow configuration, dashboard and visualization building, third-party integration setup, data uploads, template creation, workflow customization, and general troubleshooting.

 - Communicate clearly and responsively with customers, owning all correspondence from initial contact through resolution.

 - Handle sensitive information, including athlete health data, and enforce data security measures to protect all information.

 - Coordinate with internal stakeholders across Product and Engineering to surface bugs and customer feedback, providing thorough documentation when escalating, and ensuring products are improving and customer needs are being met.

 - Contribute to internal process improvements that keep team KPIs healthy as the business scales.

 - Participate in a rotating schedule for weekend coverage during high-stakes customer projects.


WHAT I’M LOOKING FOR


WHAT YOU MUST BRING:

 - Hands-on experience building and using Teamworks performance products in a professional or collegiate performance environment

 - Background in sports performance: sports science, strength & conditioning, nutrition, athletic training, or a related field.

 - Strong familiarity with athlete management systems, medical software, and/or Teamworks AMS.

 - Demonstrated ability to solve complex problems independently and with urgency, often without a complete set of information.

 - Data literacy: ability to manage and structure performance data, recognize patterns and anomalies, and apply that insight to troubleshooting and workflow construction.

 - Experience with Salesforce, JIRA (or other ticketing/support systems), MS Excel, and G-Suite.

 - Comfortable handling sensitive athlete health data with sound judgment and adherence to data security practices.

 - Bachelor's degree.


EVEN BETTER IF:

 - Experience in the athletics industry, collegiate or professional.

 - Experience with data analysis and visualization tools such as R, Python, Tableau, or similar.

 - Bilingual (Spanish is a plus).


WHY THIS ROLE

You don't wait to be told what to do — you log in, assess your queue, and get to work. You'll find the answer even when the question is half-formed

notes: Late-stage sports tech company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles
